asset_name: Player leaf — Perfect air-brake glow burst
shared_properties: The 32x32px player footprint, stem placement, and core lobe structure stay aligned with character_player_leaf_idle_spin_8f_10fps.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #E8722A, background #D4A96A, and accent #F5D060.
The center vein remains the main structural cue for motion readability.
differences: A gold halo and brighter vein highlight communicate the strongest timing result immediately.
The lower edge compresses inward to sell the temporary air-brake force window.
Frame cadence is faster than the idle loop so the perfect feedback feels punchy during the 0.4 second slowdown.

context.usage: Shown on a PERFECT Space input when the player compresses air and slows the fall speed.

shape.silhouette: The base leaf silhouette gains a short gold halo and a compressed downward posture.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 128x32px with 4 frames of 32x32px and 0px spacing.
Each frame includes a readable gold halo using #F5D060 or #FFF4CC that does not exceed the 32x32px bounds.
The pivot and body silhouette align with character_player_leaf_idle_spin_8f_10fps so animation swaps do not shift the collision read.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_player_sheet
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the player leaf silhouette, glow accent, and frame density for the responsive states.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas sprite with an additive-looking glow layer baked into the atlas frames for deterministic response timing.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/character_player_leaf_perfect_air_brake_glow_4f_20fps.png with the player base atlas set and switch to this atlas for the perfect brake state window.
runtime_notes: Spawn the state on a PERFECT lane judgement and return to the idle atlas after 0.4 seconds or the next stronger state change.
Keep the sprite anchored to the same pivot as character_player_leaf_idle_spin_8f_10fps so velocity changes do not produce visible jitter.
Drive glow intensity from authored frames rather than shader pulses so replay capture stays deterministic across hardware.
